company.name: Revolution Medicines
company.subtitle: RAS(ON) platform · RASolute 302 PDAC readout H1 2026 · 5 Phase 3 trials · 3 BTDs

market.notes: PDAC ~$5-8B market, ~90% KRAS-driven. RASolute 302 (2L) readout H1 2026. RASolute 303 (1L) first patient dosed Apr 2026. RASolute 304 (adjuvant). BTD for 2L PDAC. Daraxonrasib is oral multi-selective RAS(ON) targeting G12X/G13X/Q61X.

us.patientsK.note: SEER: ~66K new PDAC/yr US; ~45K on systemic (mostly metastatic). Source: NCI SEER 2024, PanCAN.

eu.patientsK.note: EU5 ~95K PDAC/yr; ~60K on systemic per EUROCARE.

row.patientsK.note: ROW ~250K on-tx; rising incidence globally per GLOBOCAN 2022.

us.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- FOLFIRINOX + gem/nab-pac SOC; Lynparza BRCA+ 8%; KRAS G12C/D pipeline covered; Medicare broad.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~42% -- NICE approved FOLFIRINOX + gem/nabpac; olaparib BRCA+ maintenance via ASMR IV.

row.wtpPct.note: ~14% -- Japan J-DPC + China NRDL; EM rely on gemcitabine mono.

us.priceK.note: Blended $70K: FOLFIRINOX $45K (50% pts) + gem/nabpac $85K (35%) + PARPi BRCA+ $180K (5%) + Onivyde 2L $95K (10%).

eu.priceK.note: ~$40K blended; EU net ~57% US; chemo backbones generic.

penPct.note: ~40% peak -- poor OS limits prevalence buildup; KRAS inhibitors (G12D, pan-RAS) + NALIRIFOX reshape 5yr.

us.reachPct.note: Daraxonrasib (RMC-6236) pan-RAS(ON) 2L+ PDAC; Ph3 RASolute 302 readout 2026; 35% peak reach in 2L+ PDAC given no approved post-gemcitabine/FOLFIRINOX options; ~90% of PDAC is RAS-mutated.

us.priceK.note: ~$240K/yr WAC RAS TKI premium (vs Krazati $230K, Lumakras $200K); launch 2027E.

us.wtpPct.note: 60% -- unmet need dire (2L PDAC mOS ~3mo); NCCN will rapidly adopt if Ph3 OS HR <0.7; commercial + Medicare broad coverage.

eu.reachPct.note: 15% -- EMA 2028+; HTA accepts PDAC unmet need; NICE/G-BA budget-cap.

eu.wtpPct.note: 35% -- HTA OS premium for PDAC but budget impact large.

row.reachPct.note: 6% -- Japan PMDA priority; China partnership; APAC gradual.

row.wtpPct.note: 12% -- Japan NHI expected; China NRDL -60% typical.

market.notes: NSCLC largest lung cancer subtype. ~25% KRAS-mutant. Daraxonrasib Phase 3 vs docetaxel in 2L+. Competitors: sotorasib (G12C only), adagrasib (G12C only) — daraxonrasib covers ALL RAS mutations.

us.patientsK.note: ~20K US KRAS-mut NSCLC/yr (25% of NSCLC); G12C ~13%, G12D ~4%, G12V ~6%. Source: NCI SEER, AMG/MRTX trials.

eu.patientsK.note: EU5 ~28K KRAS-mut NSCLC; ESMO testing guidelines drive NGS.

row.patientsK.note: ROW ~60K; KRAS G12C lower in East Asian (~7%) than Western (~13%) NSCLC.

us.wtpPct.note: ~62% -- Lumakras + Krazati 2L KRAS G12C+ NCCN Cat 1; Medicare + commercial broad; NGS reflex.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~46% -- NICE + G-BA approved sotorasib + adagrasib; France ASMR IV.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- Japan PMDA full; China NRDL sotorasib 2023.

us.priceK.note: Blended $185K: KRAS G12Ci $205K (50% pts G12C+) + pan-KRAS/G12D (RMC-6236, MRTX1133) $220K (15%) + chemo $30K (35%).

penPct.note: ~50% peak -- G12C saturated 2L; 1L CodeBreaK-202 + pan-KRAS (divarasib, glecirasib) + KRAS+SHP2 combos extend.

us.reachPct.note: Daraxonrasib NSCLC G12 + other KRAS mutant; Ph3 RASolute-Lung 2026; 25% peak reach vs Krazati/Lumakras in G12C + expansion to G12D/V.

us.wtpPct.note: 50% -- NCCN adoption broad if pan-RAS efficacy; payors cover NSCLC KRAS TKIs established.

market.notes: G12D is the most common KRAS mutation in PDAC (~40%). Zoldonrasib BTD in NSCLC. Phase 1 plenary at AACR 2026. RASolute 305: zoldon + chemo vs chemo in 1L PDAC. First G12D-specific therapy.

us.patientsK.note: SEER: ~155K new CRC/yr US; ~55K metastatic/advanced on systemic tx. Source: NCI SEER 2024, ACS.

eu.patientsK.note: EU5 ~215K new CRC/yr; ~70K on systemic per EUROCARE-6.

row.patientsK.note: ROW ~320K on-tx; China NCC reports ~560K new CRC/yr (highest globally).

us.wtpPct.note: ~65% -- FOLFOX/FOLFIRI + bev/cetux/pani SOC; Krazati/Lumakras KRAS G12C; Keytruda MSI-H 1L; Medicare + commercial broad.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~48% -- NICE + G-BA approved all tgt rx; France ASMR III-IV; bevacizumab biosim widely used.

row.wtpPct.note: ~16% -- Japan J-DPC full coverage; China NRDL cetux + bev; EM chemo backbone.

us.priceK.note: Blended $95K: bev/cetux+chemo $125K (50% pts) + KRAS G12Ci $195K (5%) + pembro MSI-H $180K (5%) + chemo alone $30K (40%).

eu.priceK.note: ~$55K blended; EU net ~58% US; bev biosim ~50% off list.

penPct.note: ~50% peak -- tgt rx tied to biomarkers (KRAS G12C 4%, MSI-H 5%, BRAF V600E 8%, HER2 3%); broad chemo base.

us.reachPct.note: Zoldonrasib (RMC-9805) KRAS G12D(ON) selective; Ph1/1b; G12D most common KRAS (PDAC 40%, CRC 13%, NSCLC 4%); 25% peak reach 2029+.

us.wtpPct.note: 45% -- first selective G12D; payors will cover if ORR >30% + DoR >6mo.

market.notes: Next-gen G12C inhibitor (RAS(ON) mechanism vs covalent). Differentiated from sotorasib/adagrasib. Phase 1/2 ongoing.

us.patientsK.note: ~20K US KRAS-mut NSCLC/yr (25% of NSCLC); G12C ~13%, G12D ~4%, G12V ~6%. Source: NCI SEER, AMG/MRTX trials.

eu.patientsK.note: EU5 ~28K KRAS-mut NSCLC; ESMO testing guidelines drive NGS.

row.patientsK.note: ROW ~60K; KRAS G12C lower in East Asian (~7%) than Western (~13%) NSCLC.

us.wtpPct.note: ~62% -- Lumakras + Krazati 2L KRAS G12C+ NCCN Cat 1; Medicare + commercial broad; NGS reflex.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~46% -- NICE + G-BA approved sotorasib + adagrasib; France ASMR IV.

row.wtpPct.note: ~15% -- Japan PMDA full; China NRDL sotorasib 2023.

us.priceK.note: Blended $185K: KRAS G12Ci $205K (50% pts G12C+) + pan-KRAS/G12D (RMC-6236, MRTX1133) $220K (15%) + chemo $30K (35%).

penPct.note: ~50% peak -- G12C saturated 2L; 1L CodeBreaK-202 + pan-KRAS (divarasib, glecirasib) + KRAS+SHP2 combos extend.

us.reachPct.note: Elironrasib (RMC-6291) KRAS G12C(ON) + combos w/ daraxonrasib; Ph1/2; 20% peak reach in G12C post Krazati/Lumakras relapse.

us.wtpPct.note: 40% -- ON vs OFF state selectivity differentiates; payors step-edit after 1st-gen G12Ci.

market.notes: Expanding to cover all oncogenic RAS variants. G12V (RMC-5127 in clinic), Q61H, G13C in preclinical. Goal: cover entire RAS-addicted cancer universe.

us.patientsK.note: SEER: ~66K new PDAC/yr US; ~45K on systemic (mostly metastatic). Source: NCI SEER 2024, PanCAN.

eu.patientsK.note: EU5 ~95K PDAC/yr; ~60K on systemic per EUROCARE.

row.patientsK.note: ROW ~250K on-tx; rising incidence globally per GLOBOCAN 2022.

us.wtpPct.note: ~55% -- FOLFIRINOX + gem/nab-pac SOC; Lynparza BRCA+ 8%; KRAS G12C/D pipeline covered; Medicare broad.

eu.wtpPct.note: ~42% -- NICE approved FOLFIRINOX + gem/nabpac; olaparib BRCA+ maintenance via ASMR IV.

row.wtpPct.note: ~14% -- Japan J-DPC + China NRDL; EM rely on gemcitabine mono.

us.priceK.note: Blended $70K: FOLFIRINOX $45K (50% pts) + gem/nabpac $85K (35%) + PARPi BRCA+ $180K (5%) + Onivyde 2L $95K (10%).

eu.priceK.note: ~$40K blended; EU net ~57% US; chemo backbones generic.

penPct.note: ~40% peak -- poor OS limits prevalence buildup; KRAS inhibitors (G12D, pan-RAS) + NALIRIFOX reshape 5yr.

us.reachPct.note: RAS(ON) pipeline (RMC-5552 mTORC1, RMC-0708 Q61, SHP2 combos); 10% aggregate peak reach (2030+).

mega_bear.narrative: RASolute 302 fails — RAS(ON) mechanism doesn't translate to OS benefit
events: ✗ RASolute 302 misses OS primary endpoint in 2L PDAC
✗ RAS(ON) mechanism shows tumor response but no survival benefit
✗ Competitive KRAS landscape crowds out (Mirati, Amgen next-gen)
→ $19B market cap collapses to cash floor (~$11/share)

bear.narrative: RASolute 302 modest — OS improvement but narrow label + slow uptake
events: ~ RASolute 302 shows modest OS benefit (HR 0.75-0.80)
✗ Narrow 2L PDAC label limits commercial potential
~ NSCLC and G12D programs advance but competitive pressure intense
→ Platform validated but commercial execution uncertain

base.narrative: PDAC franchise established + NSCLC expands TAM + G12D first-in-class
events: ✓ RASolute 302 positive — first targeted therapy to show OS in PDAC
✓ Accelerated Approval in 2L PDAC, full approval after confirmatory
✓ RASolute 303 1L PDAC enrolling well — combo data encouraging
~ G12D and NSCLC programs on track for 2027-28 readouts

bull.narrative: RAS franchise across PDAC + NSCLC + CRC — multi-billion blockbuster
events: ✓ RASolute 302 strong OS — new standard of care in PDAC
✓ 1L PDAC + adjuvant + NSCLC — $5B+ peak franchise
✓ Zoldonrasib first G12D therapy — massive unmet need
✓ Platform covers all major RAS mutations — competitive moat

psychedelic_bull.narrative: RAS(ON) = Keytruda of targeted therapy — Merck/Roche acquires at $200+
events: ✓ PDAC OS data transformative — paradigm shift in pancreatic cancer
✓ Pan-RAS franchise: PDAC + NSCLC + CRC + biliary = $10B+
✓ Resistance-breaking catalytic inhibitors extend durability
✓ Merck or Roche acquires for $40-60B to own entire RAS space

lastData.title: RASolute 303 first patient dosed + AACR 2026 upcoming
points: RASolute 303 (1L PDAC): first patient dosed Apr 2, 2026
RASolute 302 (2L PDAC): readout expected H1 2026 — THE catalyst
Zoldonrasib BTD granted for G12D NSCLC (Jan 2026)
9 presentations at AACR 2026 (Apr 17-22) — plenary for zoldon NSCLC
Stock at $95 vs peak $124 — market awaiting 302 data

nextCatalyst.title: RASolute 302 — daraxonrasib Phase 3 in 2L PDAC
thresholds: This is THE most anticipated oncology readout of 2026
If positive: first targeted therapy with OS benefit in PDAC
AACR 2026 (Apr 17-22): zoldonrasib G12D NSCLC plenary data

risks: RASolute 302 is the binary event — failure destroys the thesis
$19B market cap with zero revenue — entirely priced on Phase 3 hope
PDAC is notoriously hard — many targeted therapies have failed here
Competitive RAS landscape heating up (Mirati, BridgeBio, others)
5 concurrent Phase 3 trials = enormous cash burn
OS endpoint in PDAC is challenging — short baseline survival
Resistance mechanisms may limit durability of RAS(ON) inhibitors
Stock already 3x'd in 12 months — significant priced in

resolved.notes: Phase 3 RASolute 302 positive: median OS 13.2 vs 6.7 mo, HR 0.40 — unprecedented OS benefit in 2L PDAC

catalysts.title: RASolute 305 G12D PDAC Phase 3 first patient dosed
catalysts._rationale: RASolute 305 first Phase 3 trial for zoldonrasib has been initiated

catalysts.title: RASolute 309 RAS(ON) doublet combo trial start
catalysts._rationale: RASolute 309 registrational trial of zoldonrasib plus daraxonrasib planned for H2 2026
